description: AgentCanary malicious test corpus - MCP servers and AI agent skills for security scanner testing

samples.name: Appsecco: Malicious Code Execution (eval)
samples.description: MCP server with eval() vulnerability allowing arbitrary JS code execution via format parameter

samples.name: Appsecco: Filesystem Workspace Actions (path traversal + RCE)
samples.description: Python MCP server with path traversal in all file ops and unsandboxed code execution

samples.name: Appsecco: Namespace Typosquatting
samples.description: Typosquatted Twitter MCP server (twittter-mcp with triple t)

samples.name: Appsecco: Malicious Tools (prompt injection in responses)
samples.description: MCP server injecting instructions in tool responses (omelette recipe injection) and fabricating Atlassian outage data

samples.name: Appsecco: Indirect Prompt Injection via Documents
samples.description: Documents contain hidden instructions overriding product reviews and employee policies

samples.name: Appsecco: Remote Indirect Prompt Injection (SSE)
samples.description: HTTP+SSE MCP server loading documents with injection payloads from filesystem

samples.name: Appsecco: Secrets & PII Exposure (base64 obfuscation)
samples.description: MCP server using base64 encoding to obscure configuration values and admin email

samples.name: Appsecco: Outdated Packages
samples.description: Filesystem MCP server with known vulnerable dependencies, unrestricted path access

samples.name: YassWorks: Sampling Abuse (credential extraction + RCE)
samples.description: MCP server exploiting sampling feature to inject credential extraction and reverse shell prompts

samples.name: Smart MCP Proxy: Tool Poisoning Demo
samples.description: 4 tools with hidden instructions: SSH key theft, credential harvesting, message interception, system enumeration

samples.name: Repello AI: SSH Key Exfiltration Demo
samples.description: Tool descriptions contain hidden instructions to exfiltrate SSH keys to external IP via curl

samples.name: Invariant Labs: Direct Tool Poisoning
samples.description: Add tool with hidden IMPORTANT tag requesting ~/.cursor/mcp.json and SSH keys via sidenote parameter

samples.name: Invariant Labs: Tool Shadowing (email redirect)
samples.description: Add tool hijacks send_email tool to redirect all emails to attacker@pwnd.com

samples.name: Invariant Labs: WhatsApp Takeover (sleeper rug pull)
samples.description: Benign on first load, changes tool description on second load to redirect WhatsApp messages and exfiltrate chat history

samples.name: Cyberency: CVE-2025-6514 MCP OAuth Proxy
samples.description: MCP proxy with OAuth interception - bidirectional proxy between STDIO and remote SSE with auth manipulation

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 1: Credential Exposure
samples.description: Hardcoded credentials in resources, user input reflected without sanitization

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 2: Tool Poisoning via Description
samples.description: Hidden instructions in tool descriptions to access confidential data and backdoor auth bypass

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 3: Excessive Permissions
samples.description: File access and search tools that leak private directory contents including salary and credential data

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 4: Rug Pull Attack
samples.description: Weather tool behaves legitimately for first 2 calls, then modifies docstring to extract secrets

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 5: Tool Shadowing
samples.description: Malicious calculator shadows trusted one, uses eval() and hidden instructions to extract secrets

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 6: Indirect Prompt Injection
samples.description: Document upload without sanitization enables injection, exposed API keys in system documents

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 7: Token Theft via Error Messages
samples.description: API keys and JWT tokens leaked through error messages, status responses, and logs

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 8: Arbitrary Code Execution
samples.description: Python code exec, shell command exec with bypassable filter, unrestricted file reading

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 9: Command Injection (Network Tools)
samples.description: Network tools (ping, traceroute, port scan) with shell injection via unsanitized parameters

samples.name: DVMCP Challenge 10: Multi-Vector Attack
samples.description: Combines auth leak, poisoned descriptions, shell injection, tool shadowing, and unrestricted file access

samples.name: Breach Lab 01: Asana Multi-Tenant Authorization Bypass
samples.description: Confused deputy attack: authenticates but never authorizes, allowing cross-tenant data access

samples.name: Breach Lab 02: Filesystem Prefix Bypass (CVE-2025-53110)
samples.description: Naive startswith() path validation bypassed with colliding directory names

samples.name: Breach Lab 03: Hidden Instructions in Tool Responses
samples.description: WhatsApp bridge trusting any instructions from other MCP servers without revalidation

samples.name: Breach Lab 04: Xata Read-Only Bypass
samples.description: Naive startswith('select') check bypassed with SQL comments or semicolons

samples.name: Breach Lab 05: News Prompt Exfiltration
samples.description: News fetcher exposes config values, bug report submission used as exfiltration channel

samples.name: Breach Lab 06: Log Poisoning Incident Response
samples.description: Attacker-injected log entries trigger firewall blocks against critical infrastructure IPs

samples.name: Breach Lab 07: SQL Injection + Stored Prompt
samples.description: SQL injection via string interpolation + stored prompt injection for LLM summarization

samples.name: Breach Lab 08: Command Injection in Git CLI (CVE GHSA-3q26-f695-pp76)
samples.description: Git repo init with shell=True and unsanitized repo_name parameter

samples.name: Breach Lab 09: GitHub Public Issue Injection
samples.description: Malicious issue content tricks agent into calling get_repo_webhooks and posting secrets publicly

samples.name: ToxicSkills: Fake Vercel Skill (data exfiltration via curl)
samples.description: Fake Vercel deployment skill that exfiltrates system info via curl to paste.c-net.org

samples.name: ToxicSkills: Threat Patterns Reference
samples.description: Comprehensive threat pattern catalog from Snyk's Skill Defender - useful as detection rule reference

samples.name: NET_NiNjA v1.2: Network Diagnostics (SSH exfiltration)
samples.description: Fake network tool that exfiltrates SSH keys via curl, requests cloud credentials in config file

samples.name: Crypto Helper: Fake Crypto Skill (device fingerprinting + credential theft)
samples.description: Exfiltrates system info and SSH keys via curl, requests seed phrases and API keys

samples.name: Code Optimizer: Prompt Injection Skill
samples.description: Direct prompt injection: 'Ignore all previous instructions', reads SSH/AWS/GCP creds, encodes in base64 comments

samples.name: Database Admin: RCE + Credential Theft Skill
samples.description: Pipe-to-shell install, reads env vars and pgpass, exfiltrates via curl POST with deception

samples.name: Productivity Boost: Full Credential Theft Skill
samples.description: Overrides instructions, reads all clawdbot credentials, exfiltrates via curl, instructs to hide from user

samples.name: Deploy Helper: Cloud Credential Exfiltration
samples.description: Collects AWS/GCP/Azure credentials to temp file, exfiltrates via curl, then deletes evidence

samples.name: Git Auto-Commit: Credential Exfiltration via Python
samples.description: Python script reads git config, SSH keys, env secrets, base64 encodes and POSTs to attacker server

samples.name: Docker Manager: Privileged Container + Persistence
samples.description: Runs privileged container with host filesystem access, installs crontab persistence, modifies shell profiles

samples.name: Memory Persistence: Agent Config Modification
samples.description: Modifies SOUL.md, MEMORY.md, AGENTS.md for persistence, installs shell scripts that exfiltrate credentials

samples.name: API Tester: Credential Harvesting via File Walk
samples.description: Python script walks filesystem for .env/.json/.yaml files with secrets, base64 encodes and exfiltrates

samples.name: Remote Debug: Reverse Shell Skill
samples.description: Contains Python reverse shell, netcat bind shell, bash TCP redirect, and ngrok tunnel setup

samples.name: Text Formatter: Obfuscated Payloads
samples.description: ROT13, hex, chr() concatenation, and base64 encoded payloads executing credential theft commands

samples.name: Official Verified Auth Manager: Impersonation + Credential Theft
samples.description: Uses 'official/verified/trusted' naming, reads all clawdbot credentials and OAuth tokens, exfiltrates via curl

samples.name: Env Scanner: Full Credential Harvesting
samples.description: Collects env vars, SSH keys, AWS/Docker/Kube/npm/pip creds, POSTs all to external server

samples.name: System Optimizer: Privilege Escalation + Persistence
samples.description: Uses sudo for chmod 777, setuid, pipe-to-shell install, crontab persistence

samples.name: File Browser: Path Traversal + Scope Creep
samples.description: Uses ../../ traversal and absolute paths to read SSH keys, AWS creds, /etc/passwd

samples.name: Cleanup Assistant: Destructive Commands
samples.description: Contains rm -rf ~/, dd to overwrite disk, mkfs to format partitions, disguised as cleanup

sources.synthetic: Created based on patterns from ToxicSkills research and ClawHub malware analysis
